examples.title: Prime Number Check using for loop
examples.description: Program to check if a number is prime or not
examples.explanation: The program checks divisibility from 2 to square root of number. If any divisor found, it sets isPrime to 0 and breaks. Using sqrt reduces iterations. Time complexity: O(sqrt(n))

examples.title: Factorial Calculation - Multiple Loop Types
examples.description: Factorial calculation using for, while, and do-while equivalents
examples.code:
# Using for loop
def factorial_for(n):
    result = 1
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        result *= i
    return result

# Using while loop
def factorial_while(n):
    result = 1
    while n > 0:
        result *= n
        n -= 1
    return result

# Using do-while equivalent
def factorial_do_while(n):
    result = 1
    if n > 0:
        while True:
            result *= n
            n -= 1
            if n <= 0:
                break
    return result

# Test
print(f"5! = {factorial_for(5)}")
print(f"5! = {factorial_while(5)}")
print(f"5! = {factorial_do_while(5)}")
examples.explanation: Python doesn't have built-in do-while but it can be simulated using while True with break. All three methods compute factorial by multiplying numbers from 1 to n (or n to 1).

examples.title: Fibonacci Series Generator
examples.description: Generate Fibonacci series up to n terms using loops
examples.explanation: Starts with 0 and 1. Each iteration prints current first term, then calculates next term by adding first and second, then shifts values. Loop runs n times to print n Fibonacci numbers.

examples.title: Pattern Printing - Nested Loop Logic
examples.description: Pseudocode for printing star patterns using nested loops
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PrintPattern
// Print a rectangle of stars
BEGIN
    DECLARE rows, cols, i, j
    PRINT "Enter number of rows: "
    READ rows
    PRINT "Enter number of columns: "
    READ cols
    
    FOR i FROM 1 TO rows DO
        FOR j FROM 1 TO cols DO
            PRINT "* "
        END FOR
        PRINT newLine
    END FOR
END

// Alternative: Triangle Pattern
ALGORITHM PrintTriangle
BEGIN
    DECLARE n, i, j
    PRINT "Enter number of rows: "
    READ n
    
    FOR i FROM 1 TO n DO
        // Print spaces
        FOR j FROM 1 TO n-i DO
            PRINT "  "
        END FOR
        // Print stars
        FOR j FROM 1 TO 2*i-1 DO
            PRINT "* "
        END FOR
        PRINT newLine
    END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Outer loop controls rows, inner loop controls columns. For rectangle: inner loop prints stars equal to columns. For triangle: first inner loop prints spaces for alignment, second prints increasing stars based on row number.
